text:
The U.S. women芒聙聶s national team dropped into an exhausted and joyous heap on the pitch of Parc des Princes, tears flowing after defeating Brazil 1芒聙聯0 to claim its first gold medal since 2012. Coach Emma Hayes warned that Saturday芒聙聶s Olympic final wouldn芒聙聶t be pretty. And ugly it was, with the USWNT gutting out a victory behind a brilliant goal from Mallory Swanson. Contending with heavy legs, bogged down by six games in 16 days and a limited rotation, the U.S. had to dig deep against an energized,Marta-led Brazilian side. For Hayes, overcoming fatigue芒聙聰and all that comes with it芒聙聰was all part of the plan.脗

芒聙聹I want them to suffer,芒聙聺 Hayes said ahead of the gold-medal match. 芒聙聹I want them to have that moment because I do not believe you can win without it.芒聙聺

Employing videos of ultramarathoner Courtney Dauwalter to inspire her core, which she relied on with little substitution, Hayes encouraged her team to look pain in the face and overpower it. Winning amid struggle has long been a critical part of the USWNT芒聙聶s ethos, and now Hayes has instilled a similar dogged mindset in the next generation.

芒聙聹That U.S. mentality, it芒聙聶s in us it芒聙聶s kind of like can we reach back in and really bring it out,芒聙聺 Captain Lindsey Horan said ahead of the final, referencing the iconic U.S. 99ers team.

There was a real sense that the USWNT simply refused to lose this gold-medal match芒聙聰a stubbornness best exemplified by the heroics of veteran goalkeeper Alyssa Naeher. She made severalimpressive savesto maintain a clean sheet, the most astounding of which came in stoppage time. Naeher denied Brazil a close-range header, stretching to paw away what would have been a last-minute equalizer.

ALYSSA NAEHER TO THE RESCUE. AN UNBELIEVABLE SAVE IN STOPPAGE TIME. 冒聼聵卤#ParisOlympics| 冒聼聯潞 NBC and Peacockpic.twitter.com/fsJoNKSI40

Hayes will and should be applauded for cultivating a winning mindset in only 10 games at the helm of the U.S. team. But for all the talk of winning ugly, the beauty of the front three should not go unnoticed. Amid low blocks, mid blocks, slogs and unrelenting opponents, Trinity Rodman, Sophia Smith and Swanson continuously found ways to break through.脗

On Saturday, in Paris, it was Swanson芒聙聶s turn. After a treacherous opening half, the USWNT returned from the locker room with renewed focus as the forwards began to click. A dangerous chance finally came in the 57th minute, with Swanson streaking in behind the Brazilian back line, getting on the end of a well-weighted pass from midfielder Korbin Albert, before charging into the box and slotting a low shot into the back of the net. Swanson芒聙聶s clinical go-ahead finish proved to be the decider, stifling any remaining Brazil momentum.脗

TEAM USA STRIKES FIRST芒聙聰AND IT'S MALLORY SWANSON AGAIN!1-0 IN THE GOLD MEDAL MATCH.#ParisOlympics| 冒聼聯潞 NBC and Peacockpic.twitter.com/rCEQNIbR68

It was a full-circle moment for the 26-year-old, who logged her 100th USWNT cap in the Olympic final. Swanson first joined the U.S. team as a teenage phenom, representing the U.S. in Rio in 2016. In the years to come, however, Swanson would face injury trouble, complicating her much-anticipated ascent to the top of U.S. women芒聙聶s soccer. A fixture of the USWNT in its lead-up to the 2023 World Cup, Swanson was playing some of the best football of her career before tragically tearing her left patella tendon in April. After missing last year芒聙聶s world championship in Australia, Swanson got a shot at redemption in Paris芒聙聰much to the U.S.芒聙聶s benefit.

芒聙聹She芒聙聶s such an incredible player, teammate,芒聙聺 Smith said. 芒聙聹I think she just brings the energy to this team that we have been missing. Mal, I mean, is a huge piece for us.芒聙聺

There were instances of individual brilliance scattered across the pitch for the U.S., showing up at different times throughout the Games. Naomi Girma staked her claim as the best center back in the world, rarely putting a foot wrong. Fellow defenders Crystal Dunn, Tierna Davidson and Emily Fox should be lauded, too, only letting in two goals in the tournament芒聙聶s six games.脗聽脗

Those distinct pieces of the USWNT, while dazzling, weren芒聙聶t always in step, and the tactics weren芒聙聶t always executed to precision, but still, an apparent sense of unity coated the group. In just three months, Hayes curated a new persona for the U.S., sweeping away the haunting memories from a dreadful World Cup.

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s been noted on more than one occasion what happened at last year芒聙聶s World Cup,芒聙聺 Hayes said, 芒聙聹but for us, our whole focus has been on creating our own identity.芒聙聺

With a gold medal around their necks, Hayes & Co. has certainly formed a new, but familiar identity: one of winners. Standing atop the podium in Paris, the USWNT is miraculously back on top of the world for the first time since 2019. And just like that, as quickly as it started, a new USWNT era is officially in full swing.

Clare Brennan is an associate editor for Sports Illustrated focused on women芒聙聶s sports. Before joining SI in October 2022, she worked as an associate editor at Just Women芒聙聶s Sports and as an associate producer for WDET in Detroit. Brennan has a bachelor芒聙聶s in international studies from the University of Wisconsin and a master芒聙聶s in art history from Wayne State University.

title: Relentless USWNT Caps Off Gritty Olympics Run With First Gold Medal Since 2012
description: After last year's early World Cup exit, the U.S. emerged victorious in Paris, completing a return to the top of the sport.
